checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: No AccountsReceivableNetCurrent or similar receivables concept was provided in the dataset for any period.

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=97836000 (2011-06-30) declining to InventoryNet=81693000 (2012-06-30) even as revenue nearly doubled

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueNet grew from 222284000 (FY2011) to 409223000 (FY2012), yet AccountsPayableCurrent fell over the same broader window

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: No AccountsReceivable concept, no detail on the substance of the 2013-03-25 10-K/A, and only partial FY2013 quarterly data (through Q3 FY2013, 2013-03-31) are present in the dataset.

affected_line_items: Cost of Revenue/Cost of Goods Sold
mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Filing of a Form 10-K/A on 2013-03-25 to amend the previously filed FY2012 Form 10-K (filed 2012-09-10) after the Part III proxy information was already available via the 2012-10-19 DEF 14A, suggesting the amendment addressed a substantive financial correction rather than routine incorporation of proxy disclosures; this coincides with an unusually sharp gross margin compression in the same fiscal year.

rationale_evidence.quote: GrossProfit=137272000 (FY2012, 2011-07-01 to 2012-06-30) vs SalesRevenueNet=409223000, implying ~33.5% gross margin versus ~48% in FY2011 (GrossProfit=107242000 / SalesRevenueNet=222284000)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Purchase-accounting fair-value step-up of acquired inventory/backlog from the large FY2011 acquisition (Goodwill jumped from Goodwill=4495000 (2010-06-30) to Goodwill=54474000 (2011-06-30), and IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=66039000 (2011-06-30)) amortized through cost of revenue could have distorted reported gross margin trend, masking underlying margin performance and creating a mechanical, non-operational swing in gross profit across FY2012 quarters.

rationale_evidence.quote: GrossProfit=24428000 (2011-07-01 to 2011-09-30) vs GrossProfit=40243000 (2011-10-01 to 2011-12-31) on comparable quarterly revenue bases

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Disproportionate growth in capitalized 'Other Assets, Noncurrent' (roughly 5x) versus revenue growth (~84% FY2011-to-FY2012) could indicate capitalization of costs that would normally be expensed, deferring expense recognition and overstating near-term net income/assets.

top_signals: A Form 10-K/A was filed on 2013-03-25 to amend the previously issued FY2012 Form 10-K (filed 2012-09-10), after Part III information was already covered by the 2012-10-19 DEF 14A, suggesting a substantive financial correction rather than a routine filing.
An NT 10-K (late-filing notice) was filed 2011-09-13 shortly before the FY2011 10-K was ultimately filed 2011-09-19, indicating filing-process strain.

Gross margin collapsed from ~48% (GrossProfit=107242000 / SalesRevenueNet=222284000, FY2011) to ~33.5% (GrossProfit=137272000 / SalesRevenueNet=409223000, FY2012) coincident with a large debt-funded/purchase-accounting acquisition (Goodwill rising from 4495000 to 54474000), raising questions about purchase-accounting allocations and cost recognition.

Net losses accelerated every quarter through the observable window (NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=-24130000, -29171000, -31203000 for the three quarters ended 2012-09-30, 2012-12-31, and 2013-03-31 respectively), alongside a steep decline in StockholdersEquity from 230042000 (2011-09-30) to 122045000 (2013-03-31), indicating financial distress that can increase pressure for aggressive accounting.
OtherAssetsNoncurrent grew roughly 5x (2468000 to 12418000) while revenue grew only ~84%, a disproportionate soft-asset buildup.
No AccountsReceivable data was available to test the classic revenue-recognition red flag (CL1), limiting the completeness of this assessment.
